tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Shallow zone cold water cranking: flat-sided crankbait with tight wobble, small profile, no loud rattle. Fish channel swing ends, flat edges, steep-to-shallow transitions, and rock. Even in dirty water, bass locate it by lateral line.'

KVD: 'Mid-range 6-12ft zone: round body crankbait with wide wobble and loud rattle. Must contact bottom — deflection off rock and cover is the biggest strike trigger in cold water. Fish chunk rock banks, riprap, bridge areas, bluffs.'
KVD: 'Deep zone 12-15ft: go smaller profile with tight action on 10lb fluoro. This 3XD has produced all over the country, especially on highland reservoirs where bass live deeper in winter.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Cold water crankbait colors: crawfish patterns are top choice. Red/fire craw in stained water, natural greens/browns in clear water, shad patterns when baitfish are present. Pro tip: if you catch a bass with a crawfish in its mouth, note the color and match it.'

KVD: 'Cold water cranking setup: 6.2:1 gear ratio reel, 10lb fluorocarbon, slow to medium retrieve with frequent pauses. Make long casts, parallel the bank, and let the bait reach max depth. Bottom contact is non-negotiable.'

KVD: 'Cold water cranking revolves around three depth zones: (1) Shallow transitions — flat-sided, tight wobble, no rattle; (2) Mid-range 6-12ft — round body, wide wobble, loud rattle; (3) Deep 12-15ft — smaller profile, tight action. Match the crankbait to the zone.'
